name: nashville-charts
description: Interactive Nashville Number System chord charts for musicians
long_description: An MCP App that renders interactive Nashville Number System (NNS) chord charts inside Claude Desktop. NNS is the music notation system used by professional session musicians in Nashville — chords are written as scale degree numbers (1, 4, 5, 6-) instead of letter names, making charts instantly transposable to any key. Features include color-coded chord rendering, key transposition with capo calculation, medley compatibility analysis, and set list management.

tools.name: view-chart
tools.description: Render an interactive Nashville Number System chart in the chat

tools.name: browse-charts
tools.description: Browse all charts with filtering by key, artist, or title

tools.name: transpose-chart
tools.description: Show a chart transposed to a new key with updated chord map and capo recommendation

tools.name: list-charts
tools.description: List all available Nashville Number System charts with metadata

tools.name: get-chart
tools.description: Parse a chart file and return structured JSON

tools.name: transpose
tools.description: Compute transposition data: new chord map and capo position

tools.name: manage-setlist
tools.description: Visual set list management. Shows all charts organized into set and pool.

tools.name: move-song
tools.description: Move a chart file between set/ and song_pool/ directories

tools.name: analyze-medleys
tools.description: Analyze songs for medley compatibility. Scores key, tempo, and time signature.

tools.name: score-medley
tools.description: Score two specific songs for medley compatibility

tools.name: create-chart
tools.description: Generate a pre-filled NNS chart template with chord map. Returns chart text that can be edited and saved via save-chart.

tools.name: save-chart
tools.description: Validate and save an NNS chart to the charts directory. Auto-generates filename from metadata.
